title: AWS Transfer Manager, Concurrency, and Migration · Runtime Contract: Directory Transfer Failure Policy
summary: A large-transfer service starts new work on deprecated s3/manager, accepts unstable defaults, multiplies concurrency per request, and cannot bound memory, descriptors, or partial failures. Predict the representation, storage, trust, cost, and recovery evidence before inspecting a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the directory transfer failure policy increment in transfer-manager adoption and resource budget. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, ca…
objectives: Use directory transfer with explicit traversal, path mapping, symlink, overwrite, partial-success, cancellation, and failure policy.
Choose UploadObject, DownloadObject, and GetObject by streaming, seekability, checksum, destination, and ownership requirements.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Predict Directory Transfer Failure Policy · Theory Directory Transfer Failur…
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go file-delivery team handles case 4866 during a regional dependency incident. Build deterministic Go evidence to adopt transfermanager 0.3.2 while rejecting deprecated use and bounding aggregate concurrency; do not treat an ETag, CORS result, emulator, or successful SDK call as stronger proof than it is; then change the object version and prove stale authority is rejected. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Use directory transfer with explicit traversal, path mapping, symlink, overwrite, partial-success, cancellation, and failure policy.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Directory Transfer Failure Policy contract for the supplied data.
